cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking what can be learned from the text. The options are A, B, C, or D. ECP (Enterprise Community Partners) supports Palladia by providing standards for green, affordable housing. ECP's standards include things like water conservation, energy saving, and eco-friendly materials. These standards add 2% to construction costs but are offset by lower operational costs. Mayor Bloomberg plans to create 165,000 affordable units for New Yorkers. The text mentions that 80% of NYC's greenhouse gas emissions come from buildings, with 40% of those from housing. The DHPD will require all new projects to follow ECP's green standards. Other cities like Cleveland and Denver have similar measures, but NYC's DHPD is the largest affordable housing developer. The text states that 80% of greenhouse gas emissions come from buildings, and 40% of those are from housing. Since buildings are part of daily life (like residential and commercial), this seems to support B. The emissions are due to building operations, which are part of daily life. So B might be correct. C) A great number of people in NYC don't have houses to live in. But "a great number don't have houses" might be an overstatement. The problem is more about affordability and housing for specific groups, not total lack of housing. So maybe C isn't correct.
